title: macOS offline update: Safari auto-extracts + truncates the update folder — accept the folder + name it version-first
description: On macOS the default Safari/Archive-Utility behaviour AUTO-EXTRACTS a downloaded .zip into a folder. update-from-zip.command requires a .zip path as its argument (apply-update.cjs always calls extractZip on the bundle, it cannot take a directory). So a default-Safari user ends up with a FOLDER, not a zip, and the update can't be applied without manually re-zipping. WORSE: Archive Utility truncates the extracted folder NAME when it is long — 'CubricVision-macos-arm64-update-v0.0.9' became 'CubricVision-macos-arm64-update-v0' on the M4 (2026-06-10). (Confirmed it is a LENGTH truncation, not a dot issue — the shorter install folder 'CubricVision-macos-arm64-v0.0.8' kept its full name.) Workaround used in the live test: re-zip the extracted folder with `ditto -c -k --keepParent <folder> /tmp/update.zip` then point update-from-zip.command at that. FIX options: (a) make update-from-zip.command detect when its arg is a DIRECTORY and re-zip it with ditto before invoking the applier (surgical, lives in the mac wrapper); or (b) make apply-update.cjs accept either a zip or an already-extracted directory (skip extractZip, use the dir as tmpRoot) — more general, helps all platforms; or (c) shorten the update bundle's internal folder name so Archive Utility doesn't truncate it. Recommend (a) + (b). Also affects the online update.command path if it ever lands an extracted dir. Found during MPI-8 Mac 0.0.9 acceptance. Severity: blocks the offline-update happy path for default-Safari mac users — consider for 1.0.0 if mac offline update is a shipped path. RESOLUTION (2026-06-10): shipped BOTH fix (a)+(b)+(c). (b) applier accepts a directory as the bundle (commit cbe2f22, in 0.0.10) + (a) wrappers relaxed -f->-e. (c) build-portable.mjs now wraps the update bundle in a SHORT VERSION-FIRST folder `CubricVision-v<version>` via includeRoot (commit 523c938) — the archive FILENAME stays long (updater regex intact, no online-update break) but Safari extracts to `CubricVision-v0.0.10/`, short enough not to truncate and version-visible even if it did (Fabio's idea). So the user just points update-from-zip.command at the extracted folder — no re-zip. NEEDS A REBUILD to ship (c); the folder-accept (a)+(b) already validated functionally on Windows, real-Safari path UNTESTED on Mac (Fabio re-zipped out of habit last time without realising the applier takes a folder). Next: test folder-direct on Mac with a (c)-rebuilt bundle.
